step_by_step_reasoning: First, we list the nodes precisely corresponding to the 9 entities defined in the World Model. Next, we structure the edges chronologically based on the migration steps described in the video. We split these into two logical flows: Flow 0 for metadata/data replication initialization (populating DynamoDB, continuous replication from on-prem to hot replica, and App Discovery agent gathering metrics) and Flow 1 for the orchestrated cutover (triggered from Slack by the Migration Team, communicating with the EC2 Migration Factory, calling APIs on App Discovery and CloudEndure, and finally launching the Target EC2 instance in the target account).

graph.name: Automated Migration Factory with CloudEndure
graph.notes: Versent's automated migration factory dramatically reduces migration time from 4 hours to 10 minutes. It automates the discovery of on-prem servers, configures replication targets via CloudEndure, and automates target instance launching inside of the target AWS accounts using metadata stored in DynamoDB, all driven via Slack commands.

nodes.name: ON-PREM
nodes.notes: On-premises servers represented by the stacked server icon labeled ON-PREM, running Discovery and CloudEndure agents.

nodes.name: SLACK
nodes.notes: Slack chat tool used by the migration team to trigger operations.

nodes.name: MIGRATION TEAM
nodes.notes: The migration team acting as the primary system operators.

nodes.name: APP DISCOVERY SERVICE
nodes.notes: AWS Application Discovery Service collecting performance and connection metadata.

nodes.name: DYNAMODB
nodes.notes: Amazon DynamoDB storing target configuration metadata, RTOs, and RPOs.

nodes.name: EC2
nodes.notes: The central EC2 instance acting as the Migration Factory orchestrator.

nodes.name: CLOUDENDURE
nodes.notes: CloudEndure live migration tool managing volume replication.

nodes.name: EC2 HOT REPLICA
nodes.notes: The replication destination EC2 instance receiving continuously replicated block data.

nodes.name: EC2 Target
nodes.notes: The final launched EC2 instance on the target account side of the boundary.

edges.notes: The migration team populates DynamoDB with metadata, RTOs, RPOs, target account details, and subnet configurations.
Application Discovery Service agent collects TCP connections and performance metrics from the on-premises servers.
Continuous block-level data replication from the on-premises source to the AWS EC2 replica instance.
The migration team sends a command via Slack to trigger orchestration.
Slack integration triggers API calls on the EC2 Migration Factory orchestrator.
Migration factory requests performance and connection metadata from AWS Discovery Service.
App Discovery metadata influences configuration of the replica instance.
Migration factory issues API calls to CloudEndure to manage replication.
CloudEndure manages the replication process and final cutover on the replica instance.
The migration factory launches and configures the target instance across the account boundary.
